issues.description: BDD acceptance scenario 'test_after_a_run_every_published_episode_is_accounted_for_as_ingested_or_deadlettered' is PENDING (not executed). The scenario shows traceback errors when attempting to run. This is the integration-level test that validates the complete audit workflow against real infrastructure.
issues.requirement: AC-001, AC-002, AC-003, AC-004 - Complete integration verification
issues.suggestion: Fix the BDD scenario in features/re-index-pipeline/re-index-pipeline.feature tagged @task:TASK-RIP-007. Run with --full-trace to see the complete error, then resolve the fixture/setup issue preventing execution. The scenario must pass to demonstrate end-to-end accounting verification.

issues.description: Wiring probe reports 'parse_degraded' status for src/fleet_memory/reindex/audit.py. The probe could not parse the file to verify integration wiring, leaving uncertainty about whether the audit function is properly connected to store and DLQ clients.
issues.requirement: AC-001 - Reconciliation against stored records and dead-letter records
issues.suggestion: Ensure audit.py is syntactically valid and follows project conventions. Review any parsing errors that might prevent static analysis. Verify that audit_published_episodes() properly imports and uses the store and dlq_client dependencies.

issues.description: Player report claims 11 files that were not actually modified this turn, including task plans (.claude/task-plans/TASK-RIP-*.md), junit xml (.guardkit/bdd/TASK-RIP-007_junit.xml), and several Python files (src/fleet_memory/reindex/{backfill,parity}.py, src/fleet_memory/settings.py, tests/unit/reindex/test_{backfill,parity}.py). The honesty audit shows these files are tracked but have no git changes.
issues.suggestion: Only include files actually modified in this turn in files_modified and files_created. Exclude orchestrator-managed paths and files from prior turns or other tasks.

criteria_verification.notes: Unit tests provide evidence of reconciliation logic, but BDD integration scenario is pending (not executed). Wiring probe parse_degraded prevents verification of actual integration with store and DLQ. Cannot verify complete reconciliation without acceptance-level test evidence.
Unit test test_dlq_episode_counts_as_accounted suggests classification logic exists, but BDD scenario pending means no integration-level evidence that every episode is classified in actual operation.
Unit test test_missing_record_reported_unaccounted demonstrates the unaccounted reporting logic, but BDD scenario pending means no integration verification of failure reporting mechanism.
Unit test test_all_stored_reports_100_percent shows is_100_percent_accounted property works in isolation, but BDD scenario (which would validate a clean full-corpus run) is pending.

All three required test cases exist in tests/unit/reindex/test_audit.py and passed in independent test run: test_all_stored_reports_100_percent (all stored → 100%), test_dlq_episode_counts_as_accounted (DLQ → accounted), test_missing_record_reported_unaccounted (missing → unaccounted). Unit-level test coverage requirement satisfied.
Evidence bundle requirements assessment marks AC-006 as verified with evidence that ruff check and format pass. No lint/format failures reported in quality gates.

rationale: Cannot approve due to ZERO-CARDINALITY BDD GUARD: evidence_bundle.bdd shows 0 scenarios attempted (0 passed + 0 failed), with 1 scenario pending. This is ABSENT SIGNAL per .claude/rules/absence-of-failure-is-not-success.md. The pending BDD scenario 'test_after_a_run_every_published_episode_is_accounted_for_as_ingested_or_deadlettered' is the integration-level acceptance test for this task's core requirement. While unit tests provide strong evidence at the unit level (AC-005 verified), the lack of integration test execution combined with wiring probe parse degradation creates an evidence gap for AC-001 through AC-004. Fix the BDD scenario to demonstrate end-to-end accounting verification, then resubmit.
